description: Build a mobile-first web platform for Guangdong comprehensive evaluation admissions guides, timelines, score calculation, structured interview experiences, and audited admin workflows.

userStories.title: Scaffold the Node mobile web application
userStories.description: Create the initial Node.js API and mobile-first web foundation for the student and admin experiences.
acceptanceCriteria: Initialize a Node.js web project suitable for a mobile-first student web app, admin web app, and API routes.
Add package scripts for dev, build, typecheck, lint, and test, and document them in README.
Add an environment example with DATABASE_URL, object storage placeholders, and local authentication configuration.
Use PostgreSQL as the MVP database target and document PostgreSQL full-text search as the MVP search approach.
Render a working student home route and an admin route placeholder without runtime errors.
Run the available typecheck, lint, and test commands successfully.
userStories.notes: Implemented a dependency-free Node.js mobile web/API scaffold with student home, admin placeholder, health API, required scripts, environment template, and PostgreSQL full-text search documentation.

userStories.title: Create the core data model
userStories.description: Define persistent models for users, schools, guides, timelines, formulas, experiences, interactions, reports, source documents, and ingestion runs.
acceptanceCriteria: Add database schema or migrations for users, schools, admission_guides, timeline_events, score_formulas, experiences, experience_verifications, interactions, reports, source_documents, and ingestion_runs.
Model admission guides as school plus year plus Guangdong scope with status and version fields.
Add a unique constraint preventing duplicate interaction actions for the same user and target.
Represent official data statuses as draft, pending_review, published, and archived where required.
Add schema tests or migration validation that can run without touching production data.
userStories.notes: Added a PostgreSQL core data model migration and dependency-free static migration tests covering required tables, guide versioning and Guangdong scope, official data statuses, and interaction uniqueness.

userStories.title: Add seed data and data access helpers
userStories.description: Provide sample Guangdong comprehensive evaluation data and reusable server-side data access helpers.
acceptanceCriteria: Add seed data covering at least three schools, years 2024 through 2026, guide statuses, timeline nodes, score formulas, and published interview experiences.
Ensure at least one school year has no explicit score formula so calculator hiding behavior can be tested.
Create typed data access helpers for listing and reading schools, guides, timelines, formulas, and experiences.
Student-facing helpers return only published official data and published experiences.
Unit tests verify published-only filtering and year or school filtering.
userStories.notes: Added immutable Guangdong seed data and dependency-free student-facing data access helpers with tests for published-only visibility and school/year filters.

userStories.title: Implement authentication and roles
userStories.description: Implement MVP login, sessions, user profile defaults, and role-based access control.
acceptanceCriteria: Support production phone verification login, with a documented local OTP stub allowed only for development and tests.
Store user nickname, grade, default anonymous preference, role, and account status.
Persist only user, content_reviewer, data_reviewer, and admin in users.role; treat visitors as unauthenticated users rather than a stored role.
Block limited or banned users from restricted actions according to account status.
Phone number is used only for login, security, and risk control and is never returned in public-facing responses.
Add tests proving unauthenticated users cannot submit experiences, favorite, mark useful, report, view mine timeline, or access admin APIs.
userStories.notes: Implemented dependency-free phone OTP login/session helpers with production provider hooks, local OTP stub limited to development and tests, public user projection without phone fields, active-account gates for restricted student APIs, and reviewer/admin role gates for admin APIs.

userStories.title: Build the student home page
userStories.description: Create a mobile-first home page for high school students to discover current admissions progress and important content.
acceptanceCriteria: Home page includes grade-aware entry points for high school grade one, grade two, and grade three users.
Home page shows Guangdong comprehensive evaluation annual progress, nearest deadlines, latest published guides, and latest high-quality experiences.
No admission probability prediction, ranking prediction, paid consulting, open comments, or private messaging is shown.
Page has no horizontal scrolling at 375px, 390px, and 430px viewport widths.
Verify the page in browser automation and run the available UI or component tests.
userStories.notes: Built a data-driven mobile-first student home page with grade-aware entry points, annual Guangdong progress, nearest deadlines, latest published guides, high-quality published experiences, route tests, and Playwright overflow verification at 375, 390, and 430 px.

userStories.title: Implement the school list
userStories.description: Allow students to browse and filter Guangdong comprehensive evaluation schools and guide cards.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement GET /schools with year, status, and keyword parameters for the school list API.
Add a school list page with filters for year, school keyword, guide status, application status, and school type.
Support sorting by application deadline, update time, and school name.
Each card shows school name, year, application status, key timeline nodes, formula type or availability, and whether experiences exist.
Visitors only see published guide data and never see draft or pending_review guides.
Add automated tests for API parameters, filters, sorting, and published-only behavior.
userStories.notes: Implemented published-only school guide cards with /schools JSON content negotiation, /api/schools JSON alias, browser-rendered filters for year, keyword, guide status, application status, school type, and sorting by deadline, update time, or school name.

userStories.title: Implement the school detail page
userStories.description: Create a detail page that aggregates official guide data, timeline, formula entry, majors, requirements, rules, fees, and experiences for one school.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement GET /schools/:id with a year parameter returning school base information, current-year guide, timeline, formula, and featured experiences.
School detail page accepts a year parameter and defaults to the current or latest available year.

Display school base information, guide summary, official source link, application link, timeline, score formula entry, majors, subject requirements, academic test requirements, assessment method, admission rule, fees, and contact information.
Missing key fields render as official not specified or pending supplement rather than invented content.
Featured experiences prioritize the same school, recent two years, verified status, and useful count.
Verify the page in browser automation on mobile and desktop viewports.
userStories.notes: Implemented published-only school detail JSON and HTML for /schools/:id and /api/schools/:id with current/latest year defaulting, official guide fields, timeline, formula entry or pending supplement state, featured experience ranking, tests, and mobile/desktop Playwright verification.

userStories.title: Implement admission guide details and versioning
userStories.description: Expose official guide details with source attribution, structured fields, and version history summary.
acceptanceCriteria: Add guide list and guide detail APIs matching the PRD parameters for year, schoolId, status, and keyword.
Guide detail shows official source URL, source type, source published or updated time, structured admission fields, and version summary.
Visitors can only retrieve published guide details.
Guide field updates create a new version number rather than silently overwriting the published version.
Tests cover guide detail access control, source attribution, and version increment behavior.
userStories.notes: Implemented public guide list and detail APIs with year, schoolId, status, and keyword filters; published-only access; source attribution; structured fields; version summary; and dependency-free version increment helper tests.

userStories.title: Build timelines and school favorites
userStories.description: Provide public and personalized timeline views generated from guide fields and user school favorites.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement GET /timeline with year, schoolIds, and mine parameters; mine=true requires login and returns only favorited school events.

Generate timeline events for guide publication, application start, application deadline, preliminary review result, confirmation or payment, school assessment, shortlist publication, volunteer application, and admission publication.
Unauthenticated visitors can view the full Guangdong timeline.
Logged-in users can favorite and unfavorite schools and view a mine timeline containing only favorited school events.
Each timeline node shows status as not started, active, due soon, or ended based on configured dates.
Unknown dates display as to be announced and never use fabricated dates.
MVP reminders are site-only timeline or personal-center indicators and do not send SMS, WeChat, email, or external notifications.
Tests cover full timeline, mine timeline authentication, favorite persistence, status calculation, site-only reminders, and unknown date rendering.
userStories.notes: Implemented generated public and mine timelines with required event keys, timeline statuses, unknown-date handling, site-only reminder indicators, school favorite/unfavorite persistence, tests, and browser verification.

userStories.title: Implement the score formula engine
userStories.description: Create configurable score calculation logic for different schools and years.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement POST /score/calculate with schoolId, year, and scores input and with total score, contribution breakdown, formula explanation, and source link output.
Support weighted sum formulas such as 60/30/10 and 85/15.
Support inputs with different max scores and normalize or weight them according to formula configuration.
Reject missing required scores and scores lower than 0 or greater than the configured max.
Return total score, per-input contribution, formula name, explanation, official source link, and disclaimer.
Unit tests cover 60/30/10, 85/15, custom weights, missing inputs, out-of-range inputs, and no-formula cases.
userStories.notes: Implemented published-only score calculation via POST /score/calculate and /api/score/calculate with weighted input normalization, contribution breakdowns, formula metadata, source link, disclaimer, validation errors, and formula unit tests.

userStories.title: Build the score calculator page
userStories.description: Let students choose school and year, enter scores, and view calculated comprehensive score results.
acceptanceCriteria: Calculator page lets users complete calculation in three steps: choose school and year, enter scores, and view results.
Calculator page loads the matching published formula for the selected school and year.
When no clear formula exists for a school year, the calculation form is hidden and a clear unavailable state is shown.
Form validation prevents calculation when required scores are missing or outside allowed ranges.
Result displays comprehensive score, contribution breakdown, formula explanation, official source, and disclaimer that it is not admission probability.
Verify calculator flows with browser automation and run the formula unit tests.
userStories.notes: Built the mobile-first score calculator page with school/year selection, published formula loading, unavailable no-formula state, client validation, API-backed result rendering, route tests, formula unit tests, and Playwright flow verification.

userStories.title: Implement the experience browsing experience
userStories.description: Create the public structured interview experience list and cards.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement GET /experiences with schoolId, year, stage, assessmentType, verified, and sort parameters.
Experience list supports filters for school, year, stage, assessment type, verified status, and sort order.
Sort options include newest, useful count, and verified first.
Cards show school, year, stage, assessment format, summary, verified label, useful count, and historical reference notice for experiences older than two years.
Only published experiences are visible to visitors.
Tests cover API parameters, filtering, sorting, published-only visibility, verified labels, and historical reference notices.
userStories.notes: Implemented public experience browsing on /experiences and /api/experiences with filters, newest/useful/verified sorting, published-only cards, verified labels, historical reference notices, route tests, data-access tests, and browser verification.

userStories.title: Implement structured experience submission
userStories.description: Allow logged-in users to submit structured interview experiences with optional anonymous display and verification materials.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement POST /experiences for logged-in users to submit structured experiences.
Only logged-in users can open and submit the experience form.

Form captures year, school, major group, candidate track, stage, shortlisted status, optional admitted status, assessment types, optional location, process, question types, preparation, difficulty score, pressure score, differentiation score, advice, anonymous preference, and optional verification material metadata.
New submissions enter pending_review and are not visible on the student side until approved.
Anonymous display never exposes phone, real name, source account, or verification materials.
Tests cover required fields, pending_review default status, anonymous output, and unauthenticated blocking.
userStories.notes: Implemented logged-in structured experience submission on /experiences/new and POST /experiences or /api/experiences with required-field validation, pending_review defaults, private verification metadata capture, anonymous sanitized output, and published-only hiding for pending submissions.

userStories.title: Add user interactions for favorites, useful votes, and reports
userStories.description: Support logged-in user interactions for schools and experiences.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement POST /favorites for favoriting schools or experiences and DELETE /favorites/:id for removing favorites.
Implement POST /experiences/:id/useful so logged-in users can mark an experience useful only once.
Implement POST /reports so logged-in users can report an experience or user with reason and description.
Logged-in users can favorite schools and experiences and remove favorites.
Unauthenticated users receive a clear login-required response for all restricted interactions.
Tests verify duplicate useful votes are prevented, favorites are persisted, reports enter pending status, and restricted actions require login.
userStories.notes: Implemented authenticated school and experience favorites, useful-once experience votes, pending reports for experiences or users, target validation, login-required responses, and route tests.

userStories.title: Build the personal center
userStories.description: Create the logged-in user center for favorites, submissions, review status, notifications, and profile preferences.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement GET /me, GET /me/favorites, and GET /me/experiences for the logged-in personal center.
Personal center shows favorited schools, favorited experiences, submitted experiences, submission review status, site notifications, and account preferences.
Users can update nickname, grade, and default anonymous preference.
Submission status labels include draft, pending_review, published, rejected, and hidden where applicable.
Phone number is never displayed in public-facing content.
Browser tests verify personal center navigation and preference update behavior.
userStories.notes: Implemented logged-in personal center APIs and page with favorites, submitted experiences, status labels, site-only notifications, account preferences, profile updates, privacy tests, and browser verification.

userStories.title: Create admin access and official guide review
userStories.description: Implement admin role protection and workflows for reviewing, publishing, returning, supplementing, and archiving official guide data.
acceptanceCriteria: All admin routes and APIs require a data_reviewer or admin role where appropriate.
Implement POST /admin/guides, POST /admin/guides/:id/submit-review, POST /admin/guides/:id/publish, and POST /admin/guides/:id/archive.
Admin guide review page lists drafts and pending_review guides.
Review page shows extracted fields beside official source attribution for manual checking.
Admins can publish, return, mark pending supplement, and archive guide versions.
Publishing, returning, marking supplement, and archiving records operator identity and operation time.
Tests prove non-admin users cannot access admin guide APIs and published guides become visible to student APIs only after approval.
userStories.notes: Implemented protected admin guide review APIs and page for draft creation, submit-review, publish, return, pending supplement, and archive operations with audit records; published guide versions become visible through student APIs only after approval.

userStories.title: Implement admin timeline and formula management
userStories.description: Allow data reviewers to manage generated timeline events and score formulas with audit-friendly controls.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin timeline page shows guide-generated events and allows manual override of date, title, and description.
Manual timeline overrides require operator identity and override reason.
Formula management supports inputs schema, max scores, weights or custom config, explanation, source URL, status, and publication.
Implement POST /admin/formulas and POST /admin/formulas/:id/publish for creating, updating, and publishing formula drafts.
Formula publication requires at least one passing sample calculation test.
Formula and timeline publish or override actions record operator identity and operation time.
Tests cover override reason requirements, formula sample validation, and published formula visibility in the calculator.
userStories.notes: Implemented protected admin timeline override and formula management APIs/pages with required override reasons, operator audit records, formula draft create/update, sample-test-gated publication, and published calculator visibility after approval.

userStories.title: Implement admin moderation workflows
userStories.description: Provide review queues for experiences, verification materials, and reports.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement GET /admin/experiences and POST /admin/experiences/:id/review for experience moderation.
Implement GET /admin/verifications and POST /admin/verifications/:id/review for verification material review.
Implement GET /admin/reports and POST /admin/reports/:id/resolve for report handling.
Content reviewers can list pending experiences and review them as approved, returned, hidden, or banned where applicable.
Sensitive content and privacy warning results are shown to reviewers before approval.

Moderation blocks or requires rewrite for content from ongoing exams, undisclosed specific original questions, true-question sales, material ghostwriting, guaranteed admission claims, external traffic scams, and personal sensitive information.
Verification material review is restricted to reviewers and admins and never exposes raw material URLs to the student side.
Report handling supports keeping, hiding, deleting, or limiting the target account with a resolution note.
Tests cover moderation status transitions, prohibited-content handling, verification visibility restrictions, and report resolution.
userStories.notes: Implemented protected admin moderation queues and APIs for experiences, verification materials, and reports with content/privacy warnings, approval blocking, audited status transitions, student-safe verification visibility, public publish/hide/delete effects, account limiting, route tests, and browser verification.

userStories.title: Build AI ingestion draft workflow
userStories.description: Implement the AI-assisted official source ingestion workflow as draft-only infrastructure.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement POST /admin/ingestion-runs, GET /admin/ingestion-runs, and GET /admin/ingestion-runs/:id.
Admins can create ingestion runs with year, school, or keyword inputs.
System stores source document candidates with URL, title, source type, fetched time, content hash, raw text asset URL, and candidate, accepted, or rejected status.
System stores extracted guide fields, timeline candidates, formula candidates, confidence score, and review notes in the ingestion run.
Official source priority is Guangdong Education Examination Authority, then CHSI/Yangguang Gaokao, then university undergraduate admissions sites, then other official sources.
Third-party information sites can only be saved as discovery clues and cannot be final authoritative sources for published fields.
AI or extraction results can create drafts only and can never publish directly to the student side.
Tests cover source priority ordering, third-party source rejection for final authority, draft-only creation, and traceability from each extracted field to source document or manual note.
userStories.notes: Implemented protected admin ingestion run APIs and page with source candidate priority/traceability, third-party discovery-only enforcement, extracted guide/timeline/formula candidate storage, confidence and review notes, draft-only guide creation, schema coverage, route/data-access tests, and browser verification.

userStories.title: Complete end-to-end quality gates
userStories.description: Add cross-feature tests and UI verification for the MVP acceptance criteria.
acceptanceCriteria: End-to-end tests cover a grade-three user favoriting a school and viewing application deadline nodes in my timeline.
End-to-end tests cover a grade-three user using a school detail calculator and viewing formula source attribution.
End-to-end tests cover a grade-two user viewing 2024 through 2026 guide changes and school experiences.
End-to-end tests cover a grade-one user entering Guangdong comprehensive evaluation basics and the school overview from the home page.
End-to-end tests cover a user submitting an experience, admin approval, and student-side display.

Data quality checks report 2024 through 2026 Guangdong guide coverage against the 95% target and verify published guides have official source link, school, year, title, and source published or updated time.
Data quality checks verify each calculable formula has a source link and explanation, and no draft or pending_review official data appears on the student side.
Mobile browser verification at 375px, 390px, and 430px shows no horizontal scrolling on core pages.
Run build, typecheck, lint, unit tests, and browser tests successfully before marking the MVP complete.
userStories.notes: Added MVP end-to-end route tests, data-quality reporting and validation, package quality scripts, and Playwright-backed mobile browser overflow verification for core pages.

userStories.title: Apply the frontend PRD design system and student shell
userStories.description: Refresh the mobile-first student UI foundation to match the frontend PRD visual direction, navigation, layout, and reusable component rules.
acceptanceCriteria: Define and apply the frontend PRD design tokens in public/styles.css, including #0F9F9A primary, #0B7F7C pressed primary, #2563EB info, #16A34A success, #F59E0B warning, #DC2626 danger, #F6F8FA page background, #FFFFFF cards, #E5E7EB borders, text colors, 8px card and control radii, and light card shadow.
Use fixed system-font typography with concise page titles, card titles, body text, helper text, and 12px status tags; do not use viewport-scaled font sizes or negative letter spacing.

Student main pages use a stable four-tab bottom navigation for Home, Schools, Experiences, and My, with current state shown by primary color icon and text, safe-area padding, and at least 44px touch targets.
Student task pages such as login, experience submission, and calculator result detail can hide the bottom navigation but must retain a clear back entry.

Student top bars follow page type rules: the home page shows product name and grade switch, list pages show page title with search or filter entry, detail pages show back entry, short title, and favorite action where relevant, and form pages show back entry, page title, and save or submit state.
Student desktop layout above 768px centers the content in a 430px to 520px column while admin pages keep a full desktop layout.

Implementation keeps the current dependency-free Node.js ESM foundation and does not introduce a frontend framework, build dependency, or external icon package; icons use existing inline HTML, CSS, or SVG patterns with accessible names.

Shared cards, status tags, empty states, loading states, buttons, toasts, and icon buttons follow the frontend PRD rules: no nested cards, card radius no more than 8px, shallow status colors, accessible icon names, and no decorative large illustrations or gradient backgrounds.
Browser verification at 375px, 390px, and 430px proves no horizontal scroll, bottom navigation does not cover primary actions, and long school or major names wrap or truncate cleanly.
userStories.notes: Implemented the frontend PRD token foundation, fixed student shell with four-tab bottom navigation, page-type top bars, task-page back entries, desktop student column constraints, route tests, and browser shell checks.

userStories.title: Redesign the student home and login experience
userStories.description: Implement the frontend PRD home screen and shared phone OTP login flow for student actions.
acceptanceCriteria: Home first screen shows only the grade-aware greeting card, four core task entries, and nearest timeline nodes; latest guides, latest experiences, and grade preparation tips appear below the first screen.
The four home task entries are Schools, Timeline, Score Calculator, and Experiences, with short mobile-first labels and clear navigation targets.

Nearest timeline nodes show one to three items, prioritize favorited school nodes for logged-in users, fall back to site-wide nodes for guests or users without favorites, and show the guest prompt for logging in to view a personal timeline.

Latest guides show three lightweight rows with school, year, status, application deadline, and source status; latest experiences show three rows with school, year, stage, assessment format, and verification label.

Home empty states use the PRD copy intent for no current guides, no clear nodes, and logged-out personal timeline access without presenting admission probability, rankings, paid consulting, comments, or private messaging.

Phone OTP login validates mainland China phone numbers, requires agreement consent before enabling login, supports a 60 second verification-code countdown, shows retryable errors for failed sends, and preserves entered values on OTP errors.

After login, the user returns to the triggering page or action, and login-triggered favorite, useful vote, report, or publish actions continue and show a lightweight toast without exposing system internals.
Route tests and browser tests cover grade-one, grade-two, grade-three, guest, and logged-in home states plus login-required action continuation.
userStories.notes: Implemented the frontend PRD student home first-screen redesign, shared phone OTP login page, login-required action continuation, route coverage, and browser coverage.

userStories.title: Redesign school browsing and school detail pages
userStories.description: Update the student school list and detail pages so students can scan official guide status, deadlines, formulas, and experiences from the frontend PRD layout.
acceptanceCriteria: School list includes top search for school name or abbreviation and visible mobile filters for year, guide status, application status, and school type where supported by existing data.
School filters update the current page without navigating away, keep the selected filters visible, support clearing filters from an empty state, and show retry UI for failed loading.

School cards show school name and abbreviation, year, guide status tag, application deadline or to-be-announced label, formula tag such as 631, 85/15, custom, or no formula, experience count, and favorite control.
Favoriting from the school list or detail page requires login; unauthenticated users see the shared login guide and successful login continues the original favorite action.

School detail header shows school name, location, year switch, favorite control, and guide status tag, followed by quick actions for official guide, application link, score calculation, and experiences with at most one primary action in view.

School detail renders information cards in PRD order: key timeline, official guide summary, score formula, admission requirements, assessment and admission, fees and consultation, and featured experiences.

Unknown official fields render as official not specified, to be announced, or pending supplement instead of blank or invented content, and unpublished current-year guides fall back to the latest published guide clearly labeled historical reference.

Long official text is collapsed by default with an expand control, official links open in a new page, and the lightweight bottom action bar prioritizes application link, score calculator, then experience submission without showing more than one primary button in the viewport.
When no published formula exists, the detail page does not expose a score calculation entry and explains that the calculation waits for official clarification.
Mobile and desktop browser verification covers the school list and detail layouts, including 375px, 390px, and 430px widths.
userStories.notes: Redesigned school list and detail pages with abbreviation search, AJAX filters with empty/retry states, favorite controls, PRD-ordered detail cards, historical fallback labeling, no-formula calculator hiding, bottom action bar, route tests, and mobile/desktop browser verification.

userStories.title: Redesign timeline and score calculator pages
userStories.description: Implement the frontend PRD timeline and score calculator interactions with clear official-source context and no admission prediction language.
acceptanceCriteria: Timeline page provides All Nodes and My Favorites tabs, year and node-type filters, month-grouped timeline sections, and node cards with school, node type, date or to-be-announced label, status tag, and source guide year.
Timeline node statuses render as not started, active, due soon, ended, or to be announced using both text and color; color is never the only status indicator.

Clicking a timeline node opens the related school detail page, unauthenticated users selecting My Favorites see the login guide, and logged-in users with no favorites see the collect-schools empty state.

Score calculator shows the school and year selector, current formula status, formula explanation with weights and max-score notes, official basis link, dynamic score inputs generated from score_formulas.inputs_schema, and the result card.

Changing school or year clears entered scores, invalid scores show inline errors near the input, required missing inputs disable calculation, and the calculate button shows loading during calculation.
When no published formula or source-backed formula exists, the calculator shows the unavailable state and hides the calculation form.

After calculation, the result card scrolls into view and shows total comprehensive score, per-input contribution, formula name, official source link, and the standard disclaimer that the result does not represent admission probability.
Calculator and timeline UI never output probability, ranking, recommended application decisions, guaranteed admission, or similar anxiety-inducing copy.
Route tests and browser automation cover all timeline states, no-favorites state, no-formula calculator state, invalid input, valid calculation, result scrolling, and source attribution.
userStories.notes: Redesigned timeline tabs, filters, month groups, status presentation, linked node cards, My Favorites login and no-favorites states, and calculator source-backed formula, validation, loading, no-formula, result scrolling, and non-probability flows with route and browser coverage.

userStories.title: Redesign experience browsing, detail, and submission flows
userStories.description: Implement the frontend PRD structured interview experience pages with privacy-safe cards, detail sections, and local draft behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Experience list includes top search across school, stage, and keyword plus visible filters for school, year, stage, assessment type, and verified status.
Experience sorting defaults to recent two years first, verified first, and updated time descending, with optional newest and useful-count sort choices.

Experience cards show school, year, major or admission group, stage, assessment format, summary, verified label, historical reference label when older than two years, useful count, and favorite control.
Pending-review experiences never appear in public lists, and empty states guide the user to change filters or publish the first relevant experience.
Experience detail presents header metadata, basic information, process, question-type categories, preparation and advice, experience ratings, and bottom actions for favorite, useful, and report.

Experience submission requires login and captures the PRD fields for basic info, result, assessment format, optional location, process, question types, preparation, advice, ratings, anonymous preference, optional verification material metadata, and publish notices.

Experience submission form shows clear required markers, character-count hints for long text fields, leave-with-unsaved-content confirmation, and verification material purpose plus reviewer-only visibility before upload.

Submission form drafts are stored locally for up to seven days, restore with a clear prompt, can be cleared in one action, are cleared on logout and successful submission, and never persist verification material files locally.

Required fields prevent submission when missing, failed submission preserves user input, successful submission goes to an under-review state, and under-review content is visible only in My Submissions.
Anonymous experience output never exposes phone, real name, source account, verification materials, or other user-identifying information.
Browser and route tests cover filtering, sorting, public-only visibility, detail actions, draft restore and expiry behavior, anonymous output, and review-pending visibility.
userStories.notes: Redesigned experience browsing, detail, and submission flows with keyword filters, PRD default sorting, privacy-safe cards/detail actions, seven-day local drafts, route tests, and browser verification.

userStories.title: Redesign the personal center
userStories.description: Update the My page to match the frontend PRD logged-out and logged-in personal-center structure.
acceptanceCriteria: Logged-out My page shows a login guide card explaining that login enables school favorites, experience publishing, and review-status tracking.
Logged-in My page shows user nickname, grade, default anonymous preference, school favorites, experience favorites, submitted experiences, site reminders, and account settings.
Submitted experience groups show pending review, published, returned, hidden, and rejected states where applicable, with student-safe labels and next actions.
Site reminders show nearest nodes for favorited schools and submission review status changes without sending SMS, WeChat, email, or other external notifications.
Account settings allow updating nickname, grade, and default anonymous preference, and provide logout that clears local experience-submission drafts.
Phone numbers, real names, source accounts, and verification material details never render in My page public-facing sections.
Browser tests cover logged-out login guide, logged-in navigation, favorites, submissions, reminders, preference updates, logout, and draft clearing.
userStories.notes: Redesigned the My page with a logged-out login guide, logged-in user/favorite/submission/reminder/settings structure, grouped student-safe submission statuses, site-only review reminders, privacy checks, and Playwright browser coverage for My-page flows.

userStories.title: Redesign admin desktop workflows
userStories.description: Update the admin pages to the frontend PRD desktop-first layout for ingestion, guide review, timeline, formulas, moderation, verification, and reports.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin pages use a desktop-first shell with left navigation, top global status bar, main content region, and drawers or right-side panels for detail and review workflows.
Admin left navigation includes Data Ingestion, Guide Review, Timeline Management, Formula Management, Experience Review, Verification Review, and Report Handling.

Data ingestion page supports creating runs with year, school, and keyword, shows run status, source count, extraction confidence, creator and time, and opens a detail drawer with source candidates, extracted fields, confidence, manual-confirmation items, and draft-guide creation.

Guide review page shows a queue table with school, year, status, source type, missing-field count, and update time, plus a detail review layout with structured fields beside official source preview or link and field-level confirmation state.
Guide publish, return, supplement, archive, hide, and similar admin actions show a student-visible preview and require or record reasons, operator identity, and operation time.

Timeline management shows generated nodes with school, year, node type, date, date precision, source, manual override state, and student-side status; manual overrides require a reason and preserve original generated data.

Formula management provides a formula list, editor, test sample area, and student-side preview; formulas require an official source link and at least one passing sample calculation before publication.

Experience moderation shows pending queue, sensitive and privacy warnings, student-side preview, and actions to approve, return for rewrite, hide, or limit accounts while blocking ongoing-exam content, undisclosed original questions, sales, ghostwriting, guaranteed admission claims, external traffic scams, and personal sensitive information.
Verification review keeps raw materials visible only in the backend, exposes only a verification label on the student side, and requires a reason when refusing verification.
Report handling shows report list, target preview, reason, history, and actions to keep, hide, delete display, limit account, or reject report, with operator and time recorded.
userStories.notes: Redesigned admin HTML workflows around a desktop shell with left navigation, global status bar, queue tables, right-side detail panels, student-visible previews, audit reason notes, report rejection, route tests, and Playwright browser coverage for all admin workflow pages.

userStories.title: Add frontend PRD acceptance gates
userStories.description: Add automated and manual-quality checks that verify the frontend PRD visual, content, accessibility, privacy, and product-boundary requirements.
acceptanceCriteria: Frontend browser checks cover core student pages at 375px, 390px, and 430px with no horizontal scroll, no overlapping text, no bottom navigation obstruction, and clean wrapping or truncation of long school and major names.

Checks or tests verify the visual direction uses blue-green primary actions, white cards on shallow gray page background, shallow status tags, no card nesting, no card radius above 8px, no large gradient backgrounds, and no complex decorative illustrations.

Frontend interaction checks verify list loading skeletons, filter empty states retain selected criteria, clear-filter controls are available, and retry controls appear after loading failures where pages perform client-side loading.

Accessibility checks verify icon buttons have accessible names, form errors use text near the input, status is expressed with text as well as color, and interactive controls meet the 44px minimum touch target.

Content checks prevent admission probability, ranking prediction, guaranteed admission, internal-news claims, paid consulting, open comments, private messaging, unpublicized original exam questions, and third-party sources labeled as official.
Privacy checks verify public and anonymous pages never expose phone numbers, real names, source accounts, verification materials, reviewer-only material URLs, or private reporter data.

Student acceptance checks verify the home first screen, stable four-tab bottom navigation, school-detail cross-entry actions, no-formula calculator hiding, pending-review experience hiding, and anonymous experience privacy.

Admin acceptance checks verify draft-only AI ingestion, official-source preview before guide publication, formula source link and sample-test gating, moderation warning and student preview, and backend-only verification materials.

Frontend PRD completion records a deterministic acceptance matrix that maps each frontend PRD section or story acceptance criterion to committed implementation surfaces, route or browser checks, negative product-boundary checks, and any saved artifacts.
Run npm test, npm run data-quality, and npm run browser-test before marking the frontend PRD complete.
userStories.notes: Added deterministic frontend PRD acceptance gates covering visual, interaction, accessibility, content, privacy, student, and admin requirements, plus a committed acceptance matrix and browser artifacts.
